question:
Study the following information carefully and
answer the questions accordingly.
 
Nine people A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H and I live in a
nine storey building, but not necessarily in the
same order. The lowermost floor is numbered 1
and topmost floor is numbered 9. There are only
two floors between B and H who live on the sixth
floor. I live just below B’s floor. There is a gap of
four floors between I and F. The number of floors
between F and H is the same as the number of
floors between I and G. There are only two floors
between G and A. E lives above D, but below C. D
does not live on the lowest floor.
 
Four of the following five are alike in a certain
way based on their positions in the above
arrangement and so form a group. Which of the
following does not belong to the group?
explanation:
There are only two floors between B and H who
live on the sixth floor.
I live just below B’s floor.
There is a gap of four floors between I and F.
The number of floors between F and H is the
same as the number of floors between I and G.
There are only two floors between G and A.

explanation:

and answer accordingly.
explanation:

immediately.

question:
Directions: Study the following data carefully
and answer accordingly.
Eight people live in an eight-story building. One
person lives on one floor. The ground floor is
numbered 1 and the top floor is numbered 8. L
lives below Y and above Q but not immediately. L
does not live on the fourth floor. Q lives on an
even numbered floor. People lives between S and
Q are more or less than two. X lives immediately
above O, who lives below V. Three people live
between S and U. Two people live between U,
who lives above the fourth floor and Y.
Find the odd one.
explanation:
1) Q lives on an even numbered floor.
2) L lives below Y and above Q but not
immediately.
3) L does not live on the fourth floor.

O lives above R’s floor but not on the topmost
floor. P lives just above M and on the even
numbered floor. S lives two floors above N, who
lives on odd numbered floor. T lives on
bottommost floor. P doesn’t live on the topmost
floor.
____ lives just below R’s floor.
explanation:
Starting point: Here, we can start with T, S and N
in order to form two cases.
Clues: S lives two floors above N, who lives on
odd numbered floor. T lives on bottommost floor.
Inference: So, N lives on either 3 or 5 floor.
Clues: P lives just above M and on the even
numbered floor. O lives above R’s floor but not
on the topmost floor. P doesn’t live on the
topmost floor.
Inference: So, case 1 would be rejected as P
cannot be placed. So, Q must be living at
topmost floor.
T lives just below R’s floor.

question: Three people live between K and N.
explanation:
There are only two floors between B and H, who
live on the sixth floor.
I live just below B’s floor.
There is a gap of four floors between I and F.
The number of floors between F and H is the
same as the number of floors between I and G.
